description: Using malloc/free in long-running firmware, where heap fragmentation eventually causes an allocation to fail on an unattended device.
body: Failure mode: firmware allocates and frees variable-size buffers over weeks of uptime; the heap fragments until a request that should fit cannot be satisfied, and the device — with no operator — faults or stops servicing events. Detection signals: failures appear only after long uptime, never on the bench; allocation returns null intermittently; free heap is sufficient but the largest free block is too small. Do not rely on dynamic allocation in long-running or real-time firmware. Safer fallback: switch to static buffers or fixed-size memory pools sized for the worst case, eliminate heap use on hot paths, and run a multi-day soak test to confirm no allocation failure.
